blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Developmental Abnormalities of the Kidneys?
CAKUT = 20–30% ของ congenital anomalies ทั้งหมดที่วินิจฉัย prenatal; สาเหตุของ 31% ของ childhood ESRD ใน US. 5 ข้อสำคัญ: (1) Unilateral agenesis ≠ benign: CKD ใน 13–30% ของผู้ใหญ่; ต้อง annual BP + proteinuria screening; (2) ARPKD: 20–30% ของ newborns ตาย respiratory failure; bilateral nephrectomy ใน 3 เดือนแรก → 63% neurological complications; (3) Potter syndrome = bilateral agenesis / severe dysplasia → oligohydramnios → pulmonary hypoplasia; (4) Ciliopathies (ARPKD, Joubert, Meckel-Gruber) พบบ่อยในทารกที่มี echogenic kidneys บน US; (5) Infant dialysis: 2-year survival ↑ถึง 80% — neonatologist ต้องรู้ indications + multidisciplinary approach.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
รู้ epidemiology ของ CAKUT: incidence, ESRD burden, prenatal diagnosis
วินิจฉัยและ manage unilateral vs bilateral kidney agenesis — OHVIRA syndrome, Potter syndrome
อธิบาย ectopic kidney + horseshoe kidney: associations, VUR risk, management
วินิจฉัย MCDK บน ultrasound; รู้ indications nephrectomy + Wilms' tumor monitoring (current guidelines: ไม่ recommend routine)
จำแนก syndromes ที่ associated กับ kidney dysplasia: Renal coloboma (PAX2), BOR (EYA1/SIX1/SIX5), VACTERL, Eagle-Barrett
เปรียบเทียบ ARPKD vs ADPKD: genetics, US appearance, neonatal management, prognosis
รู้ ciliopathies ที่มี kidney involvement: Bardet-Biedl, Joubert, Jeune, Meckel-Gruber, Nephronophthisis
อธิบาย renal tubular dysgenesis: genes (RAS pathway), presentation, management ของ refractory hypotension
รู้ kidney teratogens: ACEi/ARB, NSAIDs, mycophenolate — trimester ที่ critical
รู้ overgrowth syndromes กับ Wilms' tumor risk: BWS, Simpson-Golabi-Behmel, Perlman — tumor surveillance schedule
อธิบาย indications + complications ของ infant peritoneal dialysis + bilateral nephrectomy ใน ARPKD
1. CAKUT — Overview และ Epidemiology

CAKUT (Congenital Anomalies of Kidney and Urinary Tract): affects 3–6 per 1000 births; responsible for 31% of all childhood ESRD in US; represent 20–30% of all prenatally diagnosed developmental anomalies
Prenatal ultrasound: allows diagnosis of asymptomatic anomalies before birth — most CAKUT detected at 18–20 week anatomy scan
Long-term outcomes depend on: amount of functional renal tissue at birth, associated urinary tract anomalies, degree of prematurity, urine output, presence of additional congenital anomalies
Infant dialysis advances: 2-year survival rates as high as 80% reported in multinational dialysis databases (van Stralen, Kidney Int 2014)

ทีม CAKUT ต้องมีใครบ้าง?
Successful management ต้องการ multidisciplinary team: neonatologist + pediatric nephrologist + pediatric urologist + surgeon + nutritionist + nurse + social worker. ที่สำคัญที่สุด: สื่อสารกับครอบครัว อย่างต่อเนื่อง — ครอบครัวแบกรับ emotional + financial burden มหาศาล.
2. Abnormalities of Kidney Number
2.1 Unilateral Kidney Agenesis (Congenital Solitary Kidney)
Mechanism: unilateral early + complete failure of ureteric bud–metanephric mesenchyme signaling
Incidence: 1 in 500–1000 children
Associated anomalies: 30–40% have other developmental anomalies; ~25% VUR; remainder: UPJ obstruction, GI, cardiac, musculoskeletal
Genital tract associations: 32–50% of ♀ with unilateral agenesis: unicornate/didelphic uterus, vaginal obstruction

OHVIRA syndrome (Obstructed Hemivagina and Ipsilateral Renal Agenesis): arrest of müllerian + mesonephric ducts at ~week 8 → pelvic mass misdiagnosis → unnecessary surgery; uterine US recommended for all girls with solitary kidney
Renal outcomes: CKD in ~13–30% of adults; proteinuria + HTN in 5–25% during childhood; hyperfiltration hypothesis → glomerulosclerosis over time
Monitoring: compensatory hypertrophy during infancy; annual BP + proteinuria screening throughout childhood; VCUG only if UTI evidence (routine VCUG now controversial)
2.2 Bilateral Kidney Agenesis — Potter Syndrome
Incidence: 1 in 3000 births; responsible for ~20% of oligohydramnios sequence (Potter syndrome)
Potter syndrome facies: low-set ears + wide-set eyes with epicanthal folds + flat nose + receding chin — from oligohydramnios-compression
Classic outcome: pulmonary hypoplasia → death within hours to days
Emerging: serial amnioinfusion → enable lung development → infants survive to PD + kidney transplant (Bienstock, Obstet Gynecol 2014; NCT03101891 ongoing)
May be isolated OR part of syndrome: brachio-oto-renal dysplasia, hereditary kidney adysplasia
3. Abnormalities of Kidney Position
3.1 Ectopic Kidney
Kidney fails to ascend from fetal pelvis to normal position; incidence 1 in 1000 (autopsy); ~90% never diagnosed; typically asymptomatic
Types: simple ectopia (ipsilateral to ureteral insertion) vs crossed ectopia (contralateral — usually fused to orthotopic kidney)
20–30% have VUR into orthotopic kidney; NOT associated with HTN, proteinuria, or CKD; follow only if reflux or obstruction present
3.2 Horseshoe Kidney
Two kidneys fused at lower poles by parenchymal or fibrous isthmus; isthmus blocks ascent past inferior mesenteric artery origin
Incidence: 1 in 400 children; 22% associated with other anomalies (vertebral, anorectal, Turner syndrome); 20–30% VUR
Complications: UTIs + kidney stones common; CKD risk dependent on degree of kidney damage (proteinuria + HTN + scarring)
4. Abnormalities of Kidney Organization
4.1 Multicystic Dysplastic Kidney (MCDK)
Mechanism: impaired nephrogenesis → branched ducts + connective tissue + undifferentiated cells; ureter not patent; kidney nonfunctional
Incidence: 1 in 4300 births; 2nd most common cause of flank mass in newborns; typically sporadic + unilateral
Ultrasound: large cysts that do NOT communicate with renal pelvis; nuclear medicine scan NOT necessary for diagnosis
Spontaneous involution: 5% no longer visible at birth; 40–55% involuted by age 5 years
Associated: 20% VUR in contralateral kidney; 40% of these = grade III–V; 5% contralateral UPJ/UVJ obstruction

Wilms' tumor: older studies associated MCDK — recent cohorts do NOT confirm association; routine tumor monitoring NOT currently recommended for isolated MCDK (Avery 2024; Eickmeyer, J Pediatr Urol 2014)
Nephrectomy: only if clear indication (HTN, concern for tumor, persistent enlargement); not routinely performed

MCDK Follow-up Protocol ย่อ
(1) Routine US monitoring ใน infancy: confirm involution + contralateral compensatory hypertrophy. (2) Annual BP + urinalysis (proteinuria) throughout childhood. (3) Lack of compensatory hypertrophy = risk factor for progressive CKD. (4) VCUG ถ้ามี UTI — routine VCUG without UTI เป็น controversial. (5) No routine tumor surveillance (กต. current guidelines 2024).
4.2 Isolated Kidney Dysplasia
Mechanism: failure of ureteric bud–mesenchyme signaling OR early urinary flow obstruction → primitive ducts + fibromuscular tissue + disorganized lobular development
Incidence: 0.1–3% of births; most common cause of childhood ESRD
US appearance: large, bright kidneys ± cysts; unilateral vs bilateral; sporadic or genetic (mutations in ITGA8, FGF20)
Bilateral severe dysplasia + oligohydramnios = Potter syndrome phenotype → pulmonary hypoplasia; consider dialysis on case-by-case basis
5. Syndromes Associated with Kidney Agenesis / Dysplasia
5.1 Key Syndromes to Know

rows: Optic nerve coloboma (dysplasia); hearing loss; CNS anomalies
Branchial arch anomalies (clefts, fistulae, cysts); preauricular pits; hearing impairment
Vertebral, Anal atresia, Cardiac, TE fistula, Limb: 3 features required
Deficient abdominal wall muscle + bilateral cryptorchidism + hypotonic bladder; MALES only (<5% females)
Cardiac malformations, hypoparathyroidism, absent thymus, facial dysmorphia

blocks.text: 5.2 Eagle-Barrett (Prune-Belly) Syndrome — Details
Triad: (1) deficient abdominal wall muscle + (2) bilateral undescended testes + (3) urinary tract anomalies (dysplasia + enlarged hypotonic bladder)
Incidence: 3.7/100,000 live male births; <5% females; 10% respiratory insufficiency (oligohydramnios + pulmonary hypoplasia)
10–25% neonatal death (respiratory failure or prematurity); 40–50% develop CKD; 15% require dialysis or transplant — often before school age
Management: intermittent catheterization or surgery for bladder drainage; VUR common; nephrology + urology follow-up critical
6. Overgrowth Syndromes และ Wilms' Tumor Risk

rows: Abdominal US q3–4 months until age 8 years
Glypican3 mutations — X-linked; similar to BWS
Same as BWS (hemihypertrophy + nevus flammeus absent)
29% develop Wilms'; half bilateral; avg age < 2 years
Aggressive surveillance; >50% die in first month (respiratory/renal failure)

blocks.text: BWS clinical features: hemihyperplasia + macroglossia + abdominal wall defects + hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia + nevus flammeus; nephromegaly most common kidney finding + nephrogenic rests (→ Wilms' risk)
7. Cystic Kidney Diseases — Ciliopathies
7.1 Ciliopathy Concept

Primary cilia: protrusions of cell membrane; sense extracellular environment; maintain coordinated cell polarity. Failure → kidney tubular cells lose polarity → kidney cysts form. Ciliopathies = diverse syndromes united by primary cilia dysfunction. Genetically highly heterogeneous; single genes often associated with multiple syndromes.
7.2 ARPKD (Autosomal Recessive Polycystic Kidney Disease)

Genetics: PKHD1 (fibrocystin); transmembrane protein; localizes to primary cilia + mitotic spindle + apical membranes of tubule/biliary cells. Also DZIP1L mutations = more moderate course (2nd gene). AR inheritance.
Incidence: 1 in 20,000; carrier rate ~1:70; hepatorenal fibrocystic syndrome
US appearance: bilaterally enlarged echogenic kidneys, poor corticomedullary differentiation; macrocysts NOT visible at birth (develop later)

Neonatal presentation: palpable kidneys + distended abdomen + abnormal renal function from birth + severe hypertension + polyuria (concentrating defect) as blood flow ↑ postnatally + fat malabsorption (abnormal bile flow)
Liver disease: ALL patients have bile duct malformation; congenital hepatic fibrosis; portal hypertension + ascending cholangitis = common complications; hepatic synthetic function normal until late
Respiratory: 20–30% die of respiratory insufficiency from enlarged kidneys compressing chest; oligohydramnios alone ≠ poor neonatal outcome
Survival: 70–75% survive newborn period; 15-year survival 67–79%; >50% need RRT during childhood; 7–10% need liver transplant

⚠️ ARPKD: Bilateral Nephrectomy ใน 3 เดือนแรก = High Neurological Risk
Burgmaier et al. Sci Rep 2020: bilateral nephrectomy ใน < 3 months → 63% neurological complications (seizures, hypoxic brain damage, severe NDD, optic neuropathy). vs 22% ถ้า 2nd nephrectomy ที่ 4–15 months; 17% ถ้า dialysis initiated ใน 3 เดือนแรก. Mechanism: absent renin production after bilateral nephrectomy → refractory hypotension (before sympathetic system matures postnatally). ดังนั้น bilateral nephrectomy ใน 3 เดือนแรก: ตัดสินใจ case-by-case เท่านั้น. May use midodrine for refractory hypotension (1 case report).
7.3 ADPKD (Autosomal Dominant Polycystic Kidney Disease)
Most common inherited kidney disease: 1 in 500–1000; PKD1 (polycystin-1): more frequent + earlier onset; PKD2 (polycystin-2): cation channel; both interact at primary cilia
Molecular mechanism: AD inheritance but recessive at cell level — requires 2nd somatic hit to form cyst (two-hit model)
2–5% present prenatally/early childhood: enlarged hyperechoic kidneys ± cysts; generally asymptomatic at birth; CKI in 8% + HTN in 19% by adolescence
Distinguishing ARPKD vs early ADPKD: screen parents for kidney cysts; normal parental US ≠ excludes ADPKD (8–10% new mutations); no diagnostic criteria for ADPKD in children < 15 years
Vascular associations (usually in adults): cerebral aneurysms, aortic dilatation, aortic dissection
Contiguous gene deletion: TSC2 + PKD1 on chr 16 → ADPKD-TSC; cysts earlier/larger/more numerous; ESRD before age 30
7.4 Other Ciliopathies — Neonatal Relevance

rows: Structural + functional; ESRD 10%; cysts + tubulointerstitial; echogenic kidneys
Retinitis pigmentosa + obesity + polydactyly + hypogonadism + learning disabilities
Triad: cystic dysplasia + occipital encephalocele + polydactyly; >80% fetal death; only ~1/3 survive 1st week
Cystic dysplasia or nephronophthisis; ESRD in teens
Molar tooth sign (MRI); developmental delay + hypotonia + episodic apnea/tachypnea + abnormal eye movements
40% cystic dysplasia/hydronephrosis; renal failure → leading cause death after age 3
Narrow chest + short ribs + short digits; 50% die < 6 months (respiratory failure)
Corticomedullary cysts + tubulointerstitial fibrosis → ESRD early adolescence; infantile form → ESRD ~1 year
Polyuria + polydipsia from age 6 years (usually not neonatal)

blocks.text: 8. Renal Tubular Dysgenesis + Kidney Teratogens
8.1 Renal Tubular Dysgenesis (RTD)
Genetics: AR; mutations in RAS genes (AGT, REN, ACE, AGTR1) → failure of proximal tubule development
Similar pathology from: cardiac anomaly, renal artery stenosis, TTTS, ACEi/ARB use in T2/T3
Presentation: severe oligohydramnios at 20–22 weeks → oligohydramnios sequence; kidneys may appear normal or hyperechoic on US
Diagnosis: kidney biopsy — absent/dramatically reduced differentiated proximal tubules

Outcome: most die in utero or neonatal period (pulmonary hypoplasia + refractory hypotension). Of 150 reported cases: 10 survivors past 18 months; 4 with ESRD, 5 with CKD. Fludrocortisone may improve BP; FFP helpful if angiotensinogen mutation
8.2 Kidney Teratogens

rows: Inhibit nephrogenesis in T2/T3; ↓ AngII-driven tubular growth
Renal tubular dysgenesis, UPJ obstruction, kidney agenesis
↓ prostaglandin E2 → renal hypoperfusion → vasomotor nephropathy
Fetal/neonatal AKI; oligohydramnios; highest risk during nephrogenesis
FDA pregnancy category D; avoid T2/T3
Kidney + urinary tract abnormalities especially T1 exposure
Highest risk if present in first trimester

blocks.text: 9. Inborn Errors of Metabolism กับ Kidney
9.1 Key Metabolic Diseases With Renal Involvement

rows: Kidney dysplasia + cysts; NB often die weeks–months
Lethargy + 'sweaty feet' odor + nonketotic hypoglycemia + metabolic acidosis; treat with riboflavin + L-carnitine
Delayed growth + ptosis + short nose + syndactyly 2nd–3rd toes; adrenocortical deficiency possible
PEX1 (12 pexin genes) — AR; peroxisome dysfunction
Small kidney cysts + calcific stippling of epiphyses
Severe hypotonia + high forehead + triangular mouth; liver dysfunction > 3 months; death < 1 yr common
Multiple genes — AR; glycan synthesis defects
Enlarged hyperechoic kidneys + tubular microcysts; congenital nephrotic syndrome (diffuse mesangial sclerosis)
Developmental delay + seizures + hypotonia + liver disease; management individualized

blocks.text: 10. Neonatal Dialysis — When, How, Outcomes
10.1 Indications และ Approach
Consider dialysis for infants with adequate lung development (amniotic fluid sufficient for lung growth) + bilateral severe kidney disease + realistic survival expectations
Decision = multidisciplinary + family-centered; goals-of-care discussion early and openly; some conditions (e.g., Meckel-Gruber, severe ARPKD without lung) → palliative only
Peritoneal dialysis (PD): modality of choice in neonates; 2-year survival up to 80% (van Stralen, Kidney Int 2014); continuous monitoring for PD peritonitis, fluid balance, nutrition
10.2 ARPKD: Bilateral Nephrectomy Decision
Indications: enlarged kidneys causing respiratory failure, intractable HTN, feeding intolerance
Risk: absence of renin after bilateral nephrectomy → refractory hypotension (renin = key early BP regulator while sympathetic system matures)
Burgmaier Sci Rep 2020: nephrectomy < 3 months → 63% neurological complications; recommend deferring if possible to 4–15 months of age
Kidney function in ARPKD may improve over first months of life — don't rush to bilateral nephrectomy
11. NICU Practical — CAKUT ที่ NICU

Prenatal US ขึ้นว่า echogenic kidneys: คิดถึง ARPKD (bilateral echogenic ± no macrocysts), ADPKD (family history), nephronophthisis (less echo), CDG, หรือ Zellweger — ส่ง pediatric nephrology consult day 1
Oligohydramnios + bilateral renal abnormalities: assess lung development → decide early (ก่อน birth) เรื่อง resuscitation level; consult palliative care if pulmonary hypoplasia severe

ARPKD นวบ: (1) respiratory support priority; (2) ACEi for HTN; (3) fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, E, K) ถ้า fat malabsorption; (4) high fluid ถ้า polyuria; (5) ชะลอ bilateral nephrectomy ไว้อย่างน้อยถึง 4 months
Potter syndrome (bilateral agenesis): ถ้า prenatal serial amnioinfusion performed → prepare for survival pathway (PD + transplant); ถ้าไม่ได้ทำ → palliative care discussion
Unilateral agenesis นวบ: asymptomatic แต่ annual BP + proteinuria screening ตลอดชีวิต; ♀ → uterine US screening for müllerian anomalies (OHVIRA)
Beckwith-Wiedemann นวบ: hypoglycemia management priority; abdominal US q3–4 months ถึง age 8 ปี (Wilms' tumor surveillance) — อย่าลืม tumor screening ใน healthy-looking infant
Renal teratogens: ถ้า mother took ACEi/ARB ใน T2/T3 → assess neonate for oliguria + AKI + oligohydramnios; ถ้า NSAIDs (indomethacin) → monitor UO + Cr ใน 48–72 hr แรก
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Bilateral Echogenic Kidneys บน US = Start ARPKD Workup
Bilateral enlarged echogenic kidneys without macrocysts ใน neonates = ARPKD ก่อน. PKHD1 mutation → collecting duct dilation → microcysts → echogenicity แต่ macrocysts ยังไม่มีตอน newborn. ส่ง nephrology consult + genetic testing ทันที. แยกจาก ADPKD ด้วย parental US screening + family history.

PEARL 2 — Potter Syndrome ≠ Only Bilateral Agenesis
Potter sequence = ผลของ severe oligohydramnios: bilateral agenesis (20%), ARPKD, severe bilateral dysplasia, bilateral obstruction. Low-set ears + wide-spaced eyes + flat nose + receding chin = oligohydramnios facies. Cause ≠ always agenesis. ระบุ cause ที่แท้จริงเสมอ เพราะ prognosis + management แตกต่างกัน (agenesis vs severe ARPKD vs dysplasia).

PEARL 3 — MCDK ≠ Benign Without Follow-Up
MCDK involute 40–55% by age 5 แต่ contralateral kidney ต้องมี compensatory hypertrophy. Lack of compensatory hypertrophy = risk for progressive CKD. Annual BP + proteinuria ตลอด childhood. Wilms' tumor: ไม่ต้อง routine surveillance ตาม current guidelines 2024 — recent cohorts ไม่ confirm association. Nephrectomy = only if HTN, concern, or persistence.

PEARL 4 — ARPKD: Don't Nephrectomize in First 3 Months
Bilateral nephrectomy < 3 months → 63% neurological complications (Burgmaier 2020): seizures, hypoxic brain damage, severe NDD, optic neuropathy — อิสระจาก hypotensive episodes ที่รุนแรง. Mechanism: absent renin → refractory hypotension ขณะ sympathetic system ยังไม่ mature. ชะลอ nephrectomy ถึง 4–15 months ถ้าเป็นไปได้. Kidney function in ARPKD may improve in first months of life.

PEARL 5 — Beckwith-Wiedemann Infant ต้อง Abdominal US q3–4 Months
BWS: nephrogenic rests (embryonic kidney tissue) = high Wilms' risk. 10% develop embryologic cancers; 40–60% = Wilms' tumor. Abdominal US q3–4 months ถึง age 8 years: even healthy-looking BWS infant. อย่าลืม Simpson-Golabi-Behmel (similar risk) + Perlman (29% Wilms', half bilateral, average age < 2 yrs — most severe prognosis).
13. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

van Stralen KJ et al. Kidney Int 2014;86:168: Survival and clinical outcomes of children starting RRT in neonatal period — multinational database; 2-year survival up to 80% in infant dialysis; PD is primary modality.

Burgmaier K et al. Sci Rep 2020;10(1): Bilateral nephrectomy in ARPKD neonates < 3 months → 63% neurological complications vs 22% at 4–15 months and 17% with dialysis-first approach. Mechanism: absent renin → hypotension while sympathetic system immature.

Brenner BM et al. Kidney Int 1996;49:1774 (Brenner hypothesis): Hyperfiltration theory — low nephron number drives glomerular hypertension → sclerosis → nephron loss cycle. Still the dominant framework for CKD risk in CAKUT.

Guay-Woodford LM et al. J Pediatrics 2014;165:611 (ARPKD consensus): Expert consensus: diagnosis criteria, management approach (nephrectomy timing, HTN treatment, nutrition), bilateral nephrectomy caution.
Sanna-Cherchi S et al. Kidney Int 2009;76:528: Renal outcome in children with CAKUT — amount of functional renal tissue at diagnosis is the strongest predictor of long-term renal outcome.

Schwartz R, Hildebrandt F. N Engl J Med 2011;364:1533: Ciliopathies review — primary cilia dysfunction as unifying concept for diverse cystic kidney syndromes; genetic heterogeneity + clinical overlap defined.
Eickmeyer AB et al. J Pediatr Urol 2014;10:655: Natural history of MCDK — limited follow-up may be warranted in uncomplicated cases; no confirmed Wilms' tumor association in modern cohorts.

Westland R et al. Nephrol Dial Transplant 2013;28:1844: Systematic review — unilateral renal agenesis associated with higher proteinuria + HTN in childhood; CKD in 13–30% of adults; hyperfiltration-mediated injury confirmed.
Mussa A et al. Eur J Med Genet 2015;59:52 (Italian BWS consensus): BWS management — abdominal US q3–4 months until age 8 years for tumor surveillance; 10% embryologic cancer rate; 40–60% Wilms'.

Bienstock JL et al. Obstet Gynecol 2014;124:413: Successful in utero intervention for bilateral renal agenesis — serial amnioinfusion → infant survived to PD + kidney transplant; proof-of-concept for NCT03101891.

Gubler MC. Pediatr Nephrol 2014;29:51: Renal tubular dysgenesis — 150 reported cases; AR mutations in RAS genes; most die neonatal period; fludrocortisone + FFP (angiotensinogen mutations) as rescue therapy.

Reidy KJ, Rosenblum ND. Semin Nephrol 2009;29:321: Cell and molecular biology of kidney development — comprehensive review of ureteric bud branching, mesenchyme induction, and genetic control of nephron formation.
14. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: ARPKD vs ADPKD: แยกด้วย US findings และ genetics ได้อย่างไร?

ตอบ: ARPKD: PKHD1; bilateral enlarged echogenic kidneys + poor corticomedullary differentiation; macrocysts ไม่เห็นตอน newborn; AR. ADPKD: PKD1/PKD2; AD; macroscopic cysts visible (แม้บางครั้งไม่ชัดใน infants); family history parent(s) มี PKD. คัดกรอง: screen parents for kidney cysts; normal parental US ≠ excludes ADPKD (8–10% new mutations). ใน neonates แยกยาก — genetic testing ช่วยได้.
Q: ARPKD bilateral nephrectomy ใน 3 เดือนแรก: เพราะอะไรถึง avoid? กรณีไหนถึง justify?

ตอบ: Burgmaier Sci Rep 2020: < 3 months → 63% neurological complications (seizures, hypoxic brain damage, NDD, optic neuropathy). Mechanism: absent renin post-bilateral nephrectomy → refractory hypotension ขณะ sympathetic nervous system ยัง immature. กรณีที่ justify: respiratory failure + intractable HTN + feeding intolerance ที่ไม่ respond to medical management. ตัดสินใจ case-by-case; kidney function may improve first months of life.
Q: Potter syndrome คืออะไร? สาเหตุอะไรได้บ้าง?

ตอบ: Potter sequence = ผลจาก severe oligohydramnios: compression → low-set ears + wide-spaced eyes + epicanthal folds + flat nose + receding chin + pulmonary hypoplasia. สาเหตุ: bilateral kidney agenesis (20% ของ cases), bilateral MCDK, severe bilateral dysplasia, bilateral urinary obstruction, ARPKD. ระบุ underlying cause เสมอ — prognosis ต่างกัน (agenesis = worse vs severe ARPKD ที่อาจรอด). Treatment: serial amnioinfusion + dialysis + transplant ใน selected cases.
Q: Beckwith-Wiedemann syndrome นวบ: features อะไร? Wilms' tumor ต้อง monitor อย่างไร?

ตอบ: BWS: hemihyperplasia + macroglossia + abdominal wall defects + hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia + nevus flammeus; nephromegaly most common kidney finding; nephrogenic rests → Wilms' risk. Genetics: IC1/IC2 methylation abnormalities or paternal UPD 11p15.5. 10% develop embryologic cancers before age 10; 40–60% = Wilms' tumor. Monitor: abdominal US every 3–4 months until age 8 years (Mussa consensus 2015).
Q: Eagle-Barrett (prune belly) syndrome: triad คืออะไร? outcomes?

ตอบ: Triad: deficient abdominal wall musculature + bilateral undescended testes + urinary tract anomalies (dysplasia + enlarged hypotonic bladder). Incidence: 3.7/100,000 live ♂ births. 10–25% neonatal death (respiratory). 40–50% develop CKD; 15% need dialysis or transplant before school age. VUR common → UTI risk → intermittent catheterization or surgery for bladder drainage critical. Genetics: unknown; familial recurrence suggests genetic cause; autosomal CNVs identified.
Q: Renal tubular dysgenesis: ตรวจ histology เจออะไร? management ของ refractory hypotension คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Histology: absence or dramatic reduction of differentiated proximal tubules. Cause: AR mutations in RAS genes (AGT, REN, ACE, AGTR1) OR interrupted fetal renal blood flow (cardiac anomaly, TTTS, ACEi/ARB use). Presentation: oligohydramnios 20–22 weeks → oligohydramnios sequence. Refractory hypotension (absent renin): fludrocortisone ± FFP (if angiotensinogen mutation). Prognosis: most die; 10 survivors >18 months reported.
Q: MCDK: ต้อง tumor surveillance ไหม? indications ของ nephrectomy คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Tumor surveillance: ไม่ต้อง routine — older studies associated with Wilms' but recent cohorts do NOT confirm (Eickmeyer 2014; current Avery 2024 guidelines). Nephrectomy indications: (1) persistent hypertension; (2) suspicion of tumor; (3) persistent significant enlargement. Not routine. Follow-up: annual US for involution + compensatory hypertrophy + annual BP + urinalysis. Lack of contralateral compensatory hypertrophy = CKD risk factor.
Q: Meckel-Gruber syndrome: triad คืออะไร? แยกจาก Joubert syndrome อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Meckel-Gruber triad: bilateral cystic dysplasia + occipital encephalocele + polydactyly; ± hepatic fibrosis. ≥11 cilia genes; AR; 2.6/100,000. >80% prenatally diagnosed; >80% fetal death or termination; only ~1/3 survive 1st week; longest survival 28 months. แยกจาก Joubert: Joubert = molar tooth sign ใน MRI brain; developmental delay + hypotonia + episodic apnea; ไม่มี encephalocele; milder → some survive to adolescence. Meckel-Gruber = lethal.
Day 76 Preview

Avery Chapter 76 — Developmental Abnormalities of the Genitourinary System
พรุ่งนี้: Developmental Abnormalities of the Genitourinary System — ต่อยอดจาก renal development + CAKUT; เนื้อหา: obstructive uropathy (UPJ / UVJ obstruction), vesicoureteral reflux (VUR), posterior urethral valves (PUV), bladder exstrophy, ureterocele, hypospadias, differences of sex development (DSD) — บทนี้เชื่อมกับ nephrology + urology + endocrine จะ overlap กับ Ch85 (DSD) ด้วย.
